cards.front: What is an array in programming?
cards.back: A data structure that stores a collection of elements of the same data type in a single variable.
An array that stores elements in a single row or column.
An array that stores elements in multiple rows and columns.
Arrays are initialized by assigning values to individual elements or using the array literal syntax.

cards.front: What is the purpose of initializing arrays?
cards.back: Initializing arrays ensures that the array is populated with valid data and prevents runtime errors.
